effect: Opens the Quest sharing panel when the activity is available.

effect: Starts and stops the built-in Quest recorder through visible UI.

risk: passive; raw report can include app/package names and UI text

risk: passive; raw report can include shell display/window dumps and UI text
effect: Records shell display/window command witnesses, an instrumentation hierarchy snapshot, a shell uiautomator dump hint, and accessibility window/action counts. Redacted summary carries only structural counts and display IDs.

effect: Scrolls the active settings list and reveals lower capture settings including bit rate, frame rate, image stabilization, and eye perspective.

effect: Scrolls the active settings list through Android accessibility actions.
evidence: 2026-06-14 scrollProbe returned true for ACTION_SCROLL_FORWARD and revealed advanced capture setting labels.

effect: Failed to locate or move the target in focused probes despite the node being visible to UiObject2/accessibility APIs.
evidence: 2026-06-14 scrollProbe reported UiObjectNotFoundException.

effect: Moved the active Quest settings panel in a single-display probe.
evidence: 2026-06-14 single-display shellSwipe probe changed visible text hash and exposed advanced capture labels.

sequence: adb shell input rotaryencoder -d 0 scroll --axis VSCROLL,3.0
effect: Not supported on the tested Quest OS build; shell input does not list scroll or rotaryencoder.
evidence: 2026-06-14 adb shell input usage and scrollProbe output.

risk: low; can move focus or search state instead of scrolling content
effect: DPAD_DOWN and SPACE changed visible state in focused probes, but behaved like focus/search navigation rather than clean settings-list scrolling. PAGE_DOWN and TAB produced no visible-text hash change. Prefer UiObject2 or accessibility scroll actions for reliable content scrolling.
evidence: 2026-06-14 focused keyScroll sweeps summarized through the redacted report exporter.

effect: Opens com.oculus.panelapp.settings with accessible side nav and main settings recycler.

sequence: if it reports false, inject device.swipe on side-nav lane at x=170
effect: Reaches lower side-nav entries such as Camera, Developer, and Help when AndroidX side-nav scrolling alone is insufficient.

risk: open/dump only; may expose privacy-sensitive labels in local reports
sequence: click side-nav target by resource ID
dump settings main content page summary
repeat until no movement or maxSectionScrolls
effect: Records per-page visible labels, checked-node summaries, clickable-node summaries, XML dumps, and scroll outcomes for each opened section without toggling controls.
evidence: 2026-06-14 settingsSectionCrawler sweeps over camera, developer, help, privacy_safety, passcode_security, experimental, movement_tracking, and quest_link.

surface: Meta Quest Settings > Camera main content
effect: Maps camera controls across pages: recording indicator, hidden captured controls, capture markers, auto-hide, aspect ratio, microphone audio, bit rate, frame rate, image stabilization, eye perspective, and video coding format.
evidence: 2026-06-14 settingsSectionCrawler camera sweep.

surface: Meta Quest Settings > Experimental main content
effect: Maps experimental entries across pages: reset experimental features, hide camera/call controls in apps, external microphone, screen reader, content adaptive brightness, temporal dimming, positional time warp, lying-down apps, Wi-Fi QR option, seamless multitasking, and surface keyboard.
evidence: 2026-06-14 settingsSectionCrawler experimental sweep.

surface: Meta Quest Settings selected main content
effect: Developer, Help, Privacy & safety, Passcode & security, Tracking, and Link were reachable and reported no further main-content scroll movement in the tested state.
evidence: 2026-06-14 settingsSectionCrawler selected-section sweeps.

sequence: click side-nav target by resource ID
search main settings content for literal or regex label
click nearest non-checkable same-row target with selected clickMode
effect: Safely probes non-toggle child rows and records whether the resulting content differs from the clicked page. Supported click modes are coordinate, uiObject2, accessibilityClick, and accessibilityExpand. Full accessibility-state dumps are opt-in with dumpChildAccessibility=true.
evidence: 2026-06-14 settingsChildPageProbe sweeps; action-mode support added and partially swept.

risk: allowlist only; privacy-sensitive labels possible; Help rows open external surfaces
sequence: compare per-mode clicked flag and child surface hash
effect: Separates coordinate tap behavior from live UiObject2.click(), AccessibilityNodeInfo ACTION_CLICK, and ACTION_EXPAND behavior for rows that are visible but inconclusive through coordinate tapping. Camera rows still did not expose selector surfaces; Help rows opened Help Center/SystemUX surfaces.
evidence: 2026-06-14 partial action-mode sweep; raw report kept outside repo.

risk: allowlist only; opens selector popovers; do not select a different value by default
sequence: -e childTargets camera:Aspect ratio,camera:Bit rate,camera:Frame rate,camera:Image stabilization,camera:Eye perspective
effect: Targets the row's dropdown_button or Spinner control instead of the broad settings_list_item. Opens context_menu_list options for aspect ratio, bit rate, frame rate, image stabilization, and eye perspective without selecting a value.
evidence: 2026-06-14 completed focused dropdown sweeps; raw reports kept outside repo.

surface: Meta Quest Settings dropdown/spinner option popovers
risk: passive after dropdown open; option bounds could be used for future mutations
effect: Records one row per context_menu_item with item_title text, bounds, selected, checked, and hasDefaultMarker fields. Verified clean option rows for camera aspect ratio, bit rate, frame rate, image stabilization, and eye perspective.
evidence: 2026-06-14 compact dropdown option-summary verification sweeps; raw reports kept outside repo.

surface: Meta Quest Settings > Privacy & safety > Device permissions
effect: Opens a child page with hand/body tracking, location services, spatial data, enhanced spatial services, and spatial-data deletion controls.
evidence: 2026-06-14 settingsChildPageProbe privacy sweep.

surface: Meta Quest Settings > Privacy & safety > App permissions
effect: Opens a child page with app permission categories including audio files, connected cameras, headset cameras, location, microphone, nearby devices, and photos/videos.
evidence: 2026-06-14 settingsChildPageProbe privacy sweep.

surface: Meta Quest Settings > Camera selector rows
risk: open/dump only; do not select a different value by default
sequence: Broad row target: settingsChildPageProbe targets camera:Aspect ratio,camera:Bit rate,camera:Image stabilization,camera:Eye perspective with row click modes
Dropdown target: settingsChildPageProbe targets camera:Aspect ratio,camera:Bit rate,camera:Frame rate,camera:Image stabilization,camera:Eye perspective with childTargetRole dropdown
effect: Broad rows are visible and activate successfully through coordinate, UiObject2.click(), and ACTION_CLICK, but do not expose options. Compact dropdown_button/Spinner targets expose option popovers: aspect ratio Square/Landscape/Portrait, bit rate 3/6/9/14 mbps, frame rate 30/60 fps, stabilization Off/Low/Medium/High, eye perspective Left/Right eye.
evidence: 2026-06-14 settingsChildPageProbe camera sweeps, partial row action-mode sweep, and completed focused dropdown sweeps.

risk: active external Help Center/SystemUX surfaces; not passive
effect: Help rows can open com.oculus.helpcenter. Support also surfaced SystemUX support/report dialog text in the tested run. Treat these as active external surfaces with side effects rather than passive settings child pages.
evidence: 2026-06-14 partial action-mode sweep; raw report kept outside repo.

effect: Did not reveal a distinct Quest quick-settings surface in the tested panel state.
evidence: 2026-06-14 quickSettings surfaceMap sweep.

effect: Did not reveal a distinct Quest notification shade in the tested panel state.
evidence: 2026-06-14 notifications surfaceMap sweep.

risk: low; raw XML can include UI text
sequence: parse XML node count outside the repo
effect: Writes a compressed XML hierarchy from host-side ADB shell. In the 2026-06-14 baseline pass, the host shell dump parsed successfully with fewer nodes than the instrumentation hierarchy, so treat it as a useful witness rather than exact parity.
evidence: 2026-06-14 direct host ADB probe wrote and parsed a compressed XML hierarchy; raw XML kept outside repo.

effect: Did not create the expected file reliably while instrumentation was running.
evidence: 2026-06-14 surfaceMap instrumentation probe.

risk: open/dump only; privacy-sensitive labels possible in local raw reports
sequence: scroll main settings_recycler_view until no movement or cap
effect: Reached all known top-level side-nav sections and no-move endpoints with object scrolling only. Multi-page sections observed were Camera, Movement, Experimental, and Notifications.
evidence: 2026-06-14 full settingsSectionCrawler sweep; raw report kept outside repo.

risk: open/dump only; local report can include installed app notification names
effect: Reached six notification pages and a true no-move endpoint after five successful content scrolls. Surface includes global controls, notification position, device/app categories, and per-app notification rows; do not commit local app names.
evidence: 2026-06-14 focused notifications settingsSectionCrawler endpoint sweep.

risk: low by default; mutation only with allowOptionSelect=true
effect: After opening a dropdown, finds a requested option row by text, records row bounds and selected/checked/default state, and refuses to click with reason allowOptionSelect=false dry run. Verified for aspect ratio Portrait 9:16, bit rate 9 mbps, frame rate 60 fps, image stabilization High, and eye perspective Right eye without changing settings.
evidence: 2026-06-14 guarded option-target dry-run sweeps.

effect: Converts raw UIAutomator report JSONL into public-safe page counts, scroll endpoint status, allowlisted settings labels, dropdown option evidence, child-page skip reasons, event counts, and redaction counts while omitting raw XML paths, local paths, package/resource IDs, and unknown labels such as installed app names.
evidence: 2026-06-14 exporter smoke-tested on synthetic stdin, local section/dropdown sweep reports, and a zero-node Audio child-page retry.

risk: passive; raw reports may contain privacy-sensitive labels
effect: Classifies route-like controls exposed on each settings section page as child_page, dropdown, button, or dropdown_option; records compact node evidence, nearby row texts, conservative risk buckets, and recommended follow-up probe type without clicking candidates.
evidence: 2026-06-14 focused route inventory sweep over general,camera,privacy_safety,help verified child_page and dropdown classifications plus redacted exporter summary.

surface: Meta Quest Settings low-risk child pages
risk: open/dump only; planner excludes default-blocked labels and ignores sensitive/external/mutation buckets by default
effect: Turns passive route inventory into a comma-separated childTargets list and runs a compact allowlisted child-page sweep. Verified default General-section plan opened Quick controls, Storage, and Ongoing activities. A broader low-risk plan opened Boundary, Travel mode, Vision, Mobility, Hearing, and Spatial audio for windows. Public summaries emit only safe labels and redaction counts.
evidence: 2026-06-14 generated General-section and low-risk section child-target plans with compact settingsChildPageProbe sweeps.

sequence: if prepare_android_settings has zero nodes, emit settings_*_skip instead of searching side-nav selectors
effect: Records the invisible/sleeping Settings failure mode as an explicit skip. A focused Audio child-page retry on 2026-06-14 showed android.settings.SETTINGS returning through VRShell while UIAutomator saw zero nodes; the patched child probe emitted settings_child_skip and finish rather than entering the nav search path.
evidence: 2026-06-14 focused audio:Spatial audio for windows retry after repeated settings navigation; raw report kept outside repo and summarized through Child Page Skips.

risk: passive; environment-state diagnostic; no force-stop, package kill, setting toggle, or coordinate recovery path
sequence: if prepare_android_settings has zero nodes, record focus/window-display commands, current-window dump, accessibility windows, and settings_recovery_passive_baseline
retry android.settings.SETTINGS up to retryCount with retryWaitMs
effect: Provides a standard bounded diagnostic for invisible or sleeping Settings surfaces and records whether passive baselines plus repeated Settings intent launches restore a visible accessibility tree.
evidence: 2026-06-14 visible Settings validation saw 363 XML nodes, 107 Settings-package nodes, two scrollables, and five accessibility windows. The earlier zero-node failure was not reproduced in that run, so zero-node recovery behavior remains pending.

risk: passive read-only; returned values describe local headset state and should not be copied verbatim unless needed
sequence: adb shell content call --uri content://com.oculus.rc --method GET_PROPERTY
effect: Returns a Bundle containing scriptable-testing state keys such as disable_guardian, set_proximity_close, disable_dialogs, and disable_autosleep without changing them.
evidence: 2026-06-14 read-only content-provider probe; provider present and GET_PROPERTY returned supported keys.

risk: mutating; requires explicit approval, PIN or test credentials where applicable, and rollback/reset plan
sequence: SET_PROPERTY can disable/enable boundary, blocking dialogs, and auto-sleep
SETUP_FOR_TEST configures Wi-Fi and logs in a test user
effect: Official Meta docs describe these ADB content-provider methods for automated test-device preparation. They were intentionally not run during passive capability mapping.
evidence: Official Meta Quest scriptable testing services documentation checked 2026-06-14; passive local probe also showed unknown methods return Success=false.

risk: consent UI only; does not grant result data
effect: Launches the example Activity's createScreenCaptureIntent flow, maps the visible prompt, taps the cancel role, and records RESULT_CANCELED with no result data.
evidence: 2026-06-14 MediaProjection prompt cancel sweep summarized through the redacted exporter.

risk: grants a one-use resultData token to the debug probe; no frame capture unless a future step calls getMediaProjection/createVirtualDisplay
effect: Maps the two-step Quest consent UI: target selection options are exposed, Share is disabled until the entire-view target is selected, then Share becomes enabled and returns RESULT_OK with result data. The probe does not create a virtual display, so dumpsys media_projection remains null after the run.
evidence: 2026-06-14 selected MediaProjection prompt share sweep summarized through the redacted exporter.

effect: Rejects generated APKs, raw recordings/screenshots/logs, raw UIAutomator hierarchy XML, signing material, local machine paths, serial-shaped ADB device output, raw Agent Board Quest lease serials, and common credential/private-controller tokens before commit. Curated public onboarding MP4 clips are allowed only under docs/media/*.mp4.
evidence: Added after 2026-06-14 source-audit intake and hardened after remote-operations note review to make the research-data-safety policy executable for staged changes.

risk: passive for current/quickSettings/notifications/androidSettings/metacamPanel; deeper Metacam surfaces open camera-settings menus but do not toggle or select settings
effect: Attempts known safe surface entry points, records structural node/clickable/scrollable/checked/display/package counts plus accessibility-window summaries, and reports per-surface changed/empty/error status without raw UI text or package names. Use accessibility-window active-root profiles when merged XML counts stay identical across overlapping Quest panels.
evidence: 2026-06-14 live default sweep passed: quickSettings and notifications were not distinct, androidSettings changed to Settings, and Metacam panel was best distinguished by active-window profile. Scoped Metacam sweep passed: deep settings changed the merged hierarchy and active root, while advanced settings switched the active root after scrolling.

surface: BRB Unity plus questionnaire-panel demo recording
risk: active recording; mutates temporary recorder settings only with explicit rollback; raw MP4 must stay out of repo
sequence: launch BRB Unity activity with brb.runtimeCommand=center_button before recorder start
optionally set Metacam dropdowns such as Aspect ratio=Portrait 9:16, Bit rate=14 mbps, Frame rate=60 fps, Image stabilization=High
run metacamRecordProbe with a long recordMs
send BRB runtime and questionnaire CLI commands while recorder sleeps
restore Metacam defaults Landscape 16:9, 3 mbps, 30 fps, Off
effect: Produces one continuous built-in headset recording suitable for review and later clip cutting. Landscape and portrait modes were both verified; portrait produced a true 1080x1920 source, but neither mode fully framed the current BRB plus panel composition.
evidence: 2026-06-14 BRB demo capture pass; raw recordings and UIAutomator reports kept in ignored local artifacts.
